name: go-playwright
description: Expert capability for robust, stealthy, and efficient browser automation using Playwright Go.
capabilities: Implement context-based architecture for efficient browser management
Add structured logging with Uber Zap for production observability
Create human-like interaction patterns to avoid bot detection
Handle session management with cookie save/load functionality
Debug automation scripts with headless mode and slow-motion options
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides legitimate browser automation using Playwright Go. All static findings are false positives: markdown code examples flagged as shell commands, documentation URLs flagged as network risks, and session/cookie management misinterpreted as credential access. No malicious intent detected. The skill is appropriate for web testing and automation use cases.
valueStatement: This skill enables Claude to write production-grade browser automation scripts using Playwright Go with proper architecture, logging, and human-emulation patterns for testing and web scraping.
userTitle: Write browser automation with Playwright Go
limitations: Requires Playwright drivers and browsers installed in the environment
Cannot solve CAPTCHAs - only detects and reports them
Highly sophisticated anti-bot systems may still detect automation
Full browser instances require significant RAM and CPU resources

useCases.targetUser: QA engineers and developers testing web applications
useCases.title: Automated web testing
useCases.description: Write end-to-end tests for web applications using real browsers to verify complex user interactions and dynamic content.

useCases.targetUser: Data engineers and developers building data pipelines
useCases.title: Web scraping with anti-bot bypass
useCases.description: Extract data from websites that use Cloudflare or similar protection by implementing human-emulation techniques.

useCases.targetUser: Operations teams automating manual browser workflows
useCases.title: Browser-based workflow automation
useCases.description: Automate repetitive browser tasks like form submissions, data entry, or report generation from web dashboards.

promptTemplates.title: Basic browser automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Go script using Playwright to open a browser, navigate to [URL], and print the page title.

promptTemplates.title: Login and session management
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Playwright Go script that logs into [URL] with credentials, saves the session cookies to a file, and loads them on subsequent runs.

promptTemplates.title: Human-like form filling
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a script that fills out a form on [URL] with human-like typing delays, random mouse movements, and viewport randomization to avoid detection.

promptTemplates.title: E2E test with logging
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete Playwright Go test suite for [website] that includes Zap structured logging, proper error handling with panic recovery, and context isolation for each test.

bestPractices: Always use browser contexts over launching new browser instances for better performance and isolation
Implement proper defer statements to close pages, contexts, and browsers to prevent resource leaks
Use Zap structured logging instead of fmt.Println for production debugging and traceability
antiPatterns: Avoid using Fill() for input - use Type() with random delays to simulate human typing
Do not use default timeouts - always set explicit timeouts for actions
Avoid launching a new browser for each task - reuse the browser instance with new contexts

faq.question: What is Playwright Go used for?
faq.answer: Playwright Go is a Go binding for the Playwright browser automation library. It enables programmatic control of Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit browsers for testing, scraping, and automation tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill require installing browsers?
faq.answer: Yes. You need to run 'go run github.com/playwright-community/playwright-go/cmd/playwright@latest install --with-deps' to install the required browser binaries.

faq.question: Can this skill bypass Cloudflare protection?
faq.answer: The skill includes human-emulation techniques that can bypass basic Cloudflare checks. However, extremely strict configurations or paid Cloudflare plans may still detect automation.

faq.question: How does context-based architecture improve performance?
faq.answer: Browser contexts are lightweight and can be created in milliseconds, while launching a new browser takes seconds. Reusing a single browser with multiple contexts provides isolation without the overhead of multiple processes.

faq.question: Can I use this for web scraping?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is designed for both testing and web scraping. The human-emulation features help avoid detection when scraping sites with anti-bot protection.

faq.question: What logging library does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill uses Uber Zap (go.uber.org/zap) for structured JSON logging in production and human-readable logging in development mode.
